title: phebalium glandulosum subsp. eglandulosum
intro: Phebalium glandulosum subsp. eglandulosum, also known as the Tasmanian Phebalium, is a sub-species of flowering plant in the Rutaceae family, native to Tasmania. The Tasmanian Phebalium has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Phebalium glandulosum subsp. eglandulosum can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves for topical use, taking advantage of its medicinal properties and providing relief from various health concerns.
